<style>
	#notify-bar .alert-wrapper{
		padding:5px;
	}
	#close{
		float: right;
		padding-right: 40px;
	}
	#close:hover {
		cursor: pointer;
	}
	#notify-bar .icon-wrap{
		height: 25px;
		display: inline-flex;
		width: 20px;
		margin: 5px 20px 0 0;
	}
	.notificationParagraph{
		margin-top: 16px;
		margin-bottom: 16px;
	}
	.notificationParagraph a {
		color: white;
		text-decoration: underline;
	}
	.notificationParagraph a:hover {
		color: white;
		text-decoration: underline;
	}
	.notificationParagraph a:active {
		color: white;
		text-decoration: underline;
	}
	.notificationParagraph a:focus {
		color: white;
		text-decoration: underline;
	}
	#notify-bar .notification-title{
		display: inline-flex;
		color: white;
		vertical-align: top;
		margin-top: 5px;
	}
	#notify-bar .icon-wrap svg{
		width: inherit;
		height: inherit;
		fill: white;
		padding-top:5px;
	}
	#notify-bar{
		text-align: center;
		background: #34566B;
		color: white;
	}
	@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
			#notify-bar .icon-wrap{
				float:left;
				margin: 15px 20px 0 15px;
			}
			#notify-bar .notification-title{
				display: block;
				margin-top: 5px;
				left: 50px;
				top: -30px;
				margin-right: 30px;
			}
	}

</style>

<h1 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-left has-white-color has-text-color" id="h-recovery-begins-with-a-promise">Recovery Begins with a Promise</h1>



<p class="has-text-align-left has-white-color has-text-color">Start your life in recovery with compassionate, effective treatment at Promises Behavioral Health</p>



<div class="wp-block-buttons is-horizontal is-content-justification-left is-layout-flex wp-container-core-buttons-is-layout-20be11b6 wp-block-buttons-is-layout-flex">

</div>
</div></div>



<section class="wp-block-uagb-columns uagb-columns__wrap uagb-columns__background-color uagb-columns__stack-mobile uagb-columns__valign- uagb-columns__gap-10 alignfull uagb-block-86ce0382 uagb-columns__columns-1 uagb-columns__max_width-theme"><div class="uagb-columns__overlay"></div><div class="uagb-columns__inner-wrap uagb-columns__columns-1">

<section class="wp-block-uagb-columns uagb-columns__wrap uagb-columns__background-none uagb-columns__stack-mobile uagb-columns__valign- uagb-columns__gap-10 align uagb-block-49de3545 uagb-columns__columns-2 uagb-columns__max_width-theme"><div class="uagb-columns__overlay"></div><div class="uagb-columns__inner-wrap uagb-columns__columns-2">

<p>Addiction treatment means making big changes in your life. But you don’t have to make those changes all alone.</p>



<p>Promises Behavioral Health treatment centers provide comprehensive, effective treatment services that support you throughout your entire path to recovery:</p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong><em>Addiction treatment that works. </em></strong>Our model for treatment is based on the most current evidence-based practices for substance use treatment and co-occurring diagnoses</li>



<li><strong><em>Support for doing the work of recovery.</em> </strong>Patients have the opportunity to start recovery and healing in safe, judgment-free environments where they are supported by industry-leading professionals and empathetic staff</li>
</ul>
</div>

<p>Our intake process is based on our client-first philosophy. From your very first phone call with us, we’re here to help you break down the barriers to treatment.</p>

<ul class="wp-block-list">

<li>Quick access to treatment, allowing you to start treatment without unnecessary delays or frustrations</li>
</ul>

<h2 class="wp-block-heading" id="h-recovery-is-lifelong-and-so-is-our-commitment-to-yours">Recovery is lifelong—and so is our commitment to yours.</h2>



<p>We offer a lifelong support system for individuals wherever they’re at in their recovery.</p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><a href="/rehab-admissions-process/" data-wpel-link="internal">Starting and during treatment</a></h3>



<p>At Promises Behavioral Health, we support patients even before they join us. Our entire admissions process is designed to get to know you as a person and to reduce the feeling of being overwhelmed. </p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><a href="/addiction-treatment-center-programs/alumni-program/" data-wpel-link="internal">Aftercare and alumni groups</a></h3>



<p class="has-ast-global-color-4-color has-text-color">Our extensive aftercare and alumni groups network connects you with others living in sobriety with alumni events, Facebook groups, and other forms of support to keep you focused on long-term recovery.</p>
</div>
</div></section>

				const lazyloadRunObserver = () => {
					const lazyloadBackgrounds = document.querySelectorAll( `.e-con.e-parent:not(.e-lazyloaded)` );
					const lazyloadBackgroundObserver = new IntersectionObserver( ( entries ) => {
						entries.forEach( ( entry ) => {
							if ( entry.isIntersecting ) {
								let lazyloadBackground = entry.target;
								if( lazyloadBackground ) {
									lazyloadBackground.classList.add( 'e-lazyloaded' );
								}
								lazyloadBackgroundObserver.unobserve( entry.target );
							}
						});
					}, { rootMargin: '200px 0px 200px 0px' } );
					lazyloadBackgrounds.forEach( ( lazyloadBackground ) => {
						lazyloadBackgroundObserver.observe( lazyloadBackground );
					} );
				};
				const events = [
					'DOMContentLoaded',
					'elementor/lazyload/observe',
				];
				events.forEach( ( event ) => {
					document.addEventListener( event, lazyloadRunObserver );
				} );
			</script>

Recovery Begins with a Promise

Start your life in recovery with compassionate, effective treatment at Promises Behavioral Health

 Call our 24/7 hotline 

 Verify your insurance 

There is a better future in front of you—and we’re here to help you discover it.

We help patients recover their lives after substance use disorder at our nationwide family of treatment centers.

 Promises Behavioral Health is fully accredited and certified 

 

 

 

 

About Promises Behavioral Health

With its headquarters in Brentwood TN, Promises Behavioral Health is a family of centers nationwide ready to help you achieve recovery. Our highly trained staff throughout our drug rehab centers work to fill in the gaps of treatment. By providing unique options in healing environments, Promises Behavioral Health addiction treatment changes the way we approach recovery. Whether you’re looking for treatment for yourself or for a loved one, our team is ready to help every step of the way.

 learn more about us 

 

It doesn’t matter what’s happened up to this point: there is a path to recovery for you

 Call our 24/7 hotline 

Addiction treatment means making big changes in your life. But you don’t have to make those changes all alone.

Promises Behavioral Health treatment centers provide comprehensive, effective treatment services that support you throughout your entire path to recovery:

 

 Addiction treatment that works. Our model for treatment is based on the most current evidence-based practices for substance use treatment and co-occurring diagnoses

 Support for doing the work of recovery. Patients have the opportunity to start recovery and healing in safe, judgment-free environments where they are supported by industry-leading professionals and empathetic staff

 

 

An approach to recovery that’s rooted in compassion and hope

Your story is important. Your recovery is important. This is the foundation of our relationship with our clients. 

We care about your challenges and helping you overcome them.

 Call our 24/7 hotline 

 

Our intake process is based on our client-first philosophy. From your very first phone call with us, we’re here to help you break down the barriers to treatment.

Start your recovery with:

 

A compassionate, nonjudgmental intake process to determine the right treatment schedule for you

A no-hassle insurance coverage verification process

Quick access to treatment, allowing you to start treatment without unnecessary delays or frustrations

 

Recovery is too precious to risk with unproven methods or anything less than best-in-class professionals.

Our experienced professionals are trained in current, evidence-based therapeutic methods:

 

Trauma-informed care 

Dual diagnosis treatment 

Recovery and relapse prevention

Nutrition education

Medication-assisted treatment

 

 

The addiction help you need is ready for you

 Call our 24/7 hotline 

 Medical detox 

Medical detox provides 24-hour medical monitoring for patients withdrawing from drug and alcohol use. 

We focus on ensuring your safety and comfort until you’re mentally stabilized and ready to participate in treatment.

 Residential treatment 

Now is your opportunity to dedicate your whole self towards healing. 

Promises Behavioral Health offers modern facilities with all the amenities you need to feel at home while you do the hard work of your recovery.

 Outpatient treatment 

A flexible approach to treating your drug and alcohol addiction. With our outpatient programs, you don’t have to interrupt your life to get your life back. 

Options include partial hospitalization programs, intensive outpatient programs, and continuing care groups.

 Alumni support 

Once you’ve completed your treatment program, we support you through our extensive network of alumni groups. 

Our alumni groups reinforce recovery skills, provide social and emotional support, and help advocate for addiction awareness.

Mental Health & Addiction Treatment Programs

at Promises Behavioral Health

 Call our 24/7 hotline 

 Personality Disorder 

With a residential treatment center for dependent personality disorder, many people with personality disorders can manage their symptoms.

 Anxiety Treatment 

 Our inpatient anxiety treatment centers help people overcome the effects of anxiety disorders. 

 Trauma & PTSD Treatment 

 Residential PTSD programs offer a private, secluded place where the emphasis is on treatment. 

 Non 12 Step Programs 

 Non 12 step options offer effective treatment for those who feel a 12 step approach isn’t for them. 

 Mood Disorders 

 Sex Addiction Treatment 

 Individualized treatment for sex addiction is crucial to helping clients stay safe and healthy. 

Most major insurance providers help cover the cost of treatment

Find out right now if your health insurance benefits can be used to cover substance use treatment at Promises Behavioral Health treatment centers.

 Call our 24/7 hotline 

Comprehensive, evidence-based addiction treatment centers with locations nationwide

Our effective, client-centered treatment is available no matter where you live. Our treatment centers across the country deliver specialized programs to help you achieve lifelong sobriety.

Promises Behavioral Health

Mental Health Treatment & Drug Rehab Centers

Our various locations throughout the country provide essential mental health and addiction treatment to fit the needs of each client. Promise Behavioral Health offers unique yet effective experiences for those undergoing mental health or addiction treatment. Each location is fully accredited and staffed with a knowledgeable, trained, and compassionate staff.

 see our locations 

 

 The Ranch TN 

 Nunnelly, Tennessee 

 The Ranch PA 

 Wrightsville, Pennsylvania 

 Promises Brazos Valley 

 College Station, Texas 

 The Right Step Houston 

 Houston, Texas 

 Washburn House 

 Worcester, Massachusetts 

 Promises Dallas 

Dallas, Texas

 Promises Atlanta 

 Atlanta, Georgia 

 View all Treatment Centers 

 Call our 24/7 hotline 

I need help with substance use for:

 Myself 

Asking for help is a huge accomplishment. We’re here to support individuals through all stages of their recovery from drug and alcohol addiction.

 A loved one 

Seeing a loved one struggle with the effects of addiction is painful, but you can help them by helping them access effective, compassionate treatment.

 A client 

You care deeply about helping your clients get the help they deserve. We offer evidence-based substance use disorder treatments and are committed to open, transparent communication with referring professionals.

Recovery is lifelong—and so is our commitment to yours.

We offer a lifelong support system for individuals wherever they’re at in their recovery.

 Starting and during treatment 

At Promises Behavioral Health, we support patients even before they join us. Our entire admissions process is designed to get to know you as a person and to reduce the feeling of being overwhelmed. 

 Aftercare and alumni groups 

Our extensive aftercare and alumni groups network connects you with others living in sobriety with alumni events, Facebook groups, and other forms of support to keep you focused on long-term recovery.

 

 The greatest form of courage is to ask for help 

No matter where you’re at on your journey to recovery, we’re here to help you. 

 Call our 24/7 hotline 

Social

 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 Open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week 

103 Powell Court

Suite 100

Brentwood, TN 37027

 888.483.7451

	#notify-bar .alert-wrapper{
		padding:5px;
	}
	#close{
		float: right;
		padding-right: 40px;
	}
	#close:hover {
		cursor: pointer;
	}
	#notify-bar .icon-wrap{
		height: 25px;
		display: inline-flex;
		width: 20px;
		margin: 5px 20px 0 0;
	}
	.notificationParagraph{
		margin-top: 16px;
		margin-bottom: 16px;
	}
	.notificationParagraph a {
		color: white;
		text-decoration: underline;
	}
	.notificationParagraph a:hover {
		color: white;
		text-decoration: underline;
	}
	.notificationParagraph a:active {
		color: white;
		text-decoration: underline;
	}
	.notificationParagraph a:focus {
		color: white;
		text-decoration: underline;
	}
	#notify-bar .notification-title{
		display: inline-flex;
		color: white;
		vertical-align: top;
		margin-top: 5px;
	}
	#notify-bar .icon-wrap svg{
		width: inherit;
		height: inherit;
		fill: white;
		padding-top:5px;
	}
	#notify-bar{
		text-align: center;
		background: #34566B;
		color: white;
	}
	@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
			#notify-bar .icon-wrap{
				float:left;
				margin: 15px 20px 0 15px;
			}
			#notify-bar .notification-title{
				display: block;
				margin-top: 5px;
				left: 50px;
				top: -30px;
				margin-right: 30px;
			}
	}

				const lazyloadRunObserver = () => {
					const lazyloadBackgrounds = document.querySelectorAll( `.e-con.e-parent:not(.e-lazyloaded)` );
					const lazyloadBackgroundObserver = new IntersectionObserver( ( entries ) => {
						entries.forEach( ( entry ) => {
							if ( entry.isIntersecting ) {
								let lazyloadBackground = entry.target;
								if( lazyloadBackground ) {
									lazyloadBackground.classList.add( 'e-lazyloaded' );
								}
								lazyloadBackgroundObserver.unobserve( entry.target );
							}
						});
					}, { rootMargin: '200px 0px 200px 0px' } );
					lazyloadBackgrounds.forEach( ( lazyloadBackground ) => {
						lazyloadBackgroundObserver.observe( lazyloadBackground );
					} );
				};
				const events = [
					'DOMContentLoaded',
					'elementor/lazyload/observe',
				];
				events.forEach( ( event ) => {
					document.addEventListener( event, lazyloadRunObserver );
				} );
			</script>

	#notify-bar .alert-wrapper{
		padding:5px;
	}
	#close{

		padding-right: 40px;
	}
	#close:hover {
		cursor: pointer;
	}
	#notify-bar .icon-wrap{
		height: 25px;
		display: inline-flex;
		width: 20px;
		margin: 5px 20px 0 0;
	}
	.notificationParagraph{
		margin-top: 16px;
		margin-bottom: 16px;
	}
	.notificationParagraph a {
		color: white;
		text-decoration: underline;
	}
	.notificationParagraph a:hover {
		color: white;
		text-decoration: underline;
	}
	.notificationParagraph a:active {
		color: white;
		text-decoration: underline;
	}
	.notificationParagraph a:focus {
		color: white;
		text-decoration: underline;
	}
	#notify-bar .notification-title{
		display: inline-flex;
		color: white;
		vertical-align: top;
		margin-top: 5px;
	}
	#notify-bar .icon-wrap svg{
		width: inherit;
		height: inherit;
		fill: white;
		padding-top:5px;
	}
	#notify-bar{
		text-align: center;
		background: #34566B;
		color: white;
	}
	@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
			#notify-bar .icon-wrap{
				float:left;
				margin: 15px 20px 0 15px;
			}
			#notify-bar .notification-title{
				display: block;
				margin-top: 5px;
				left: 50px;
				top: -30px;
				margin-right: 30px;
			}
	}

</style>

	<div id="primary" class="content-area primary">

		
					<main id="main" class="site-main">
				<article
class="post-395 page type-page status-publish ast-article-single" id="post-395" itemtype="https://schema.org/CreativeWork" itemscope="itemscope">
	
				<header class="entry-header ast-header-without-markup">
				<h1 class="entry-title" itemprop="headline">Medical Drug Detox Centers: Comprehensive Addiction Treatment Programs</h1>			</header> <!-- .entry-header -->
		
<div class="entry-content clear"
	itemprop="text">

	
			<div data-elementor-type="wp-page" data-elementor-id="395" class="elementor elementor-395" data-elementor-post-type="page">
						<section class="elementor-section elementor-top-section elementor-element elementor-element-483a65b elementor-section-boxed elementor-section-height-default elementor-section-height-default" data-id="483a65b" data-element_type="section">
						<div class="elementor-container elementor-column-gap-default">
					<div class="elementor-column elementor-col-100 elementor-top-column elementor-element elementor-element-41b4887b" data-id="41b4887b" data-element_type="column">
			<div class="elementor-widget-wrap elementor-element-populated">
						<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-69765a43 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="69765a43" data-element_type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
				<div class="elementor-widget-container">
									<p><img fetchpriority="high" decoding="async" class="alignright size-medium wp-image-10731" src="https://www.promises.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/Medical-Drug-Detox-Centers-300x210.jpeg" alt="a nurse consults a patient in one of the effective medical drug detox centers" width="300" height="210" srcset="https://www.promises.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/Medical-Drug-Detox-Centers-300x210.jpeg 300w, https://www.promises.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/Medical-Drug-Detox-Centers.jpeg 500w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" /></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Drug and alcohol detox is an important first step in recovering from a substance use disorder. Supervised detox helps reduce withdrawal symptoms and cravings to support recovery.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Various factors, such as the severity of addiction, health status, and individual needs, influence the duration and approach of medication-assisted treatment. The benefits of medical detox include improved safety, greater comfort during withdrawal, and better long-term recovery outcomes.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Promises</span><a href="https://www.promises.com/addiction-treatment-center-programs/" data-wpel-link="internal"> <span style="font-weight: 400;">addiction treatment programs</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> provide support for every step of the recovery journey, including detox overseen 24/7 by medical professionals. Our programs work to increase access to safe, effective detox and treatment, helping more individuals receive the care they need.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">If you’re ready to begin recovery with a strong foundation, call </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">888.289.1181</span><span style="font-weight: 400;"> to get started.</span></p>
<h2>Why Is Medical Detox Important?</h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Addiction happens because drugs change how the brain functions. If you are physically dependent on a substance, you will experience withdrawal symptoms when you stop taking it. These can include headaches, insomnia, nausea, and other extremely unpleasant and/or dangerous states.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Supervised detox is associated with better outcomes for patients, as it reduces complications and supports long-term recovery. Medical staff tracks vital signs and can administer medications to ease your symptoms. They can also intervene if any serious problems arise.</span></p>
<h2>What Is Medication-Assisted Treatment?</h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Medication-assisted treatment (MAT) is an evidence-based approach that offers hope and healing for those struggling with addictions such as opioid use disorder and alcohol use disorder. MAT uses FDA-approved medications to help minimize cravings, reduce withdrawal symptoms, and/or block the rewarding effects of substances during alcohol or opioid addiction treatment.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">MAT words best as part of a comprehensive treatment approach. At Promises treatment centers, we address not just the physical aspects of addiction, but also the emotional, social, and psychological challenges you face. Through medications, group work, and behavioral therapies, we can help you start to regain control of your health and reclaim your life.</span></p>
<h2>Benefits of MAT in Addiction Treatment</h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">It’s important to understand that medication-assisted treatment (MAT) is not simply replacing one drug with another; rather, it is a carefully managed medical approach using FDA-approved medications to support recovery.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">MAT brings hope and genuine relief to individuals facing opioid use disorder and other substance use challenges.<a href="https://www.promisesatlanta.com/addiction-treatment-georgia/detox-services-atlanta/" target="_blank" rel="noopener follow external noreferrer" data-wpel-link="external"> Effective medical detox</a> prepares you to enter alcohol or drug rehab, where you can start to explore the reasons behind the addiction. Medications for opioid use or alcohol use can reduce the risk of relapse as part of a comprehensive treatment plan.</span></p>
<h2>Medications that Treat Opioid Use Disorder and Alcohol Addiction</h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">There are several FDA-approved medications that can help make detox more comfortable and are used to treat opioid use disorder or alcohol addiction as part of a comprehensive treatment plan:</span></p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Benzodiazepines:</b><span style="font-weight: 400;"> can help ease withdrawal symptoms for alcohol detox</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Buprenorphine: </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">partial opioid agonist; blocks opioid receptors to relieve opioid cravings and reduces alcohol cravings</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Suboxone: </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">used for opioid use disorder; helps reduce cravings and prevent misuse</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Naltrexone: </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">opioid antagonist; blocks opioid receptors and prevents rewarding effects to reduce cravings for opioids and alcohol</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Methadone: </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">synthetic opioid agonist; eases withdrawal symptoms of heroin and opioids; used for severe addiction cases</span></li>
</ul>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Healthcare providers carefully prescribe these life-changing medications. They monitor effects closely and adjust treatment to ensure safety and comfort.</span></p>
<h2>Importance of Counseling in Recovery</h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Counseling forms the heart of MAT. We believe it’s essential for achieving lasting recovery from substance use disorders. Behavioral therapies like cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) and contingency management help you identify and change harmful thought patterns and behaviors and give you practical tools to manage cravings, cope with stress, and prevent relapse. Group counseling and family therapy back up the individual therapy process and strengthen your support network.</span></p>
<h2>Barriers to Accessing Evidence-Based Treatment</h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Medication-assisted treatment (MAT) transforms, and even saves, lives. Yet many people struggling with opioid use disorder face real challenges in accessing this essential care. Too few healthcare providers receive training to prescribe medications like naltrexone and buprenorphine. This shortage leaves many people without the treatment they desperately need and deserve.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Stigma and discrimination create additional hurdles for those seeking help, and some communities lack nearby treatment programs. This forces people to travel great distances or go without care entirely. We believe that increasing access to evidence-based medications and compassionate care will help us address the opioid crisis. Every person deserves the chance to heal and recover.</span></p>
<h2>Promises Medication-Assisted Treatment Locations</h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We are expanding access to evidence-based treatment through our seven drug and alcohol rehab centers:</span></p>
<p><a href="https://www.promises.com/promises-drug-addiction-treatment-centers/the-ranch-nunnelly-tn-evidence-based-treatment-center/" data-wpel-link="internal"><b>The Ranch</b></a><b> in Nunnelly, TN</b><b><br></b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Our serene, nature-focused treatment center offers evidence-based therapies and medical detox in a peaceful rural setting, ideal for those seeking a comprehensive recovery experience.</span></p>
<p><a href="https://www.promises.com/promises-drug-addiction-treatment-centers/the-ranch-wrightsville-pa-dual-diagnosis-treatment-center/" data-wpel-link="internal"><b>The Ranch</b></a><b> in Wrightsville, PA</b><b><br></b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Specializing in dual diagnosis treatment, The Ranch provides integrated care for co-occurring mental health and substance use disorders with personalized medical and therapeutic support.</span></p>
<p><a href="https://www.promises.com/promises-drug-addiction-treatment-centers/the-right-step-houston-tx-inpatient-drug-rehab-center/" data-wpel-link="internal"><b>The Right Step</b></a><b> in Houston, TX</b><b><br></b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Offering inpatient drug rehab with a focus on individualized treatment plans, The Right Step Houston combines medication-assisted treatment with counseling to support lasting recovery.</span></p>
<p><a href="https://www.promises.com/promises-drug-addiction-treatment-centers/washburn-house-worcester-ma-clinical-drug-rehab-center/" data-wpel-link="internal"><b>Washburn House</b></a><b> in Worcester, MA</b><b><br></b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Washburn House is a clinical drug rehab center that emphasizes evidence-based care and compassionate support, helping clients safely detox and transition into ongoing addiction treatment.</span></p>
<p><a href="https://www.promises.com/promises-drug-addiction-treatment-centers/promises-brazos-valley/" data-wpel-link="internal"><b>Promises Brazos Valley</b></a><b> in College Station, TX</b><b><br></b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Providing a full spectrum of addiction services, this center offers medical detox and medication-assisted treatment alongside behavioral therapies in a supportive environment.</span></p>
<p><a href="https://www.promisesatlanta.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener follow external noreferrer" data-wpel-link="external"><b>Promises Atlanta</b></a><b> in Atlanta, GA</b><b><br></b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Promises in Atlanta is a comprehensive treatment center delivering personalized care with a focus on medication-assisted treatment and holistic recovery approaches tailored to individual needs.</span></p>
<p><a href="https://www.promisesdfw.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener follow external noreferrer" data-wpel-link="external"><b>Promises Dallas</b></a><b> in Dallas, TX</b><b><br></b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Our Dallas location integrates medical detox, medication-assisted treatment, and therapy services to support clients through every stage of their recovery journey.</span></p>
<h2>Insurance Coverage for MAT</h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Most health insurance plans now provide at least partial coverage for MAT as part of substance use disorder treatment. The Affordable Care Act (ACA) requires insurance plans to cover essential health benefits, which includes treatment for substance use disorders and access to MAT. The Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA) guarantees that coverage for substance use disorder treatment, including MAT, receives the same level of support as other medical and surgical care.</span></p>
<h2>Reach out to an Evidence-Based Detox Center</h2>
<p><a href="https://www.promises.com/" data-wpel-link="internal"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Promises Behavioral Health</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> is a family of addiction and mental health treatment centers offering accredited <a href="https://www.promises.com/addiction-treatment-center-programs/medical-drug-detox-centers/" data-wpel-link="internal">medical detox programs</a> for a safer, more comfortable drug withdrawal and treatment experience. Our</span><a href="https://www.promises.com/mental-health-treatment-programs/the-ranch-inpatient-sex-addiction-treatment-centers/" data-wpel-link="internal"> <span style="font-weight: 400;">treatment centers</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> are home-like, restorative settings that also provide the latest evidence-based treatment and medications. If you’re ready to restore your physical and mental health with the aim of long-term recovery, call 888.483.7451 today.</span></p>								</div>

				const lazyloadRunObserver = () => {
					const lazyloadBackgrounds = document.querySelectorAll( `.e-con.e-parent:not(.e-lazyloaded)` );
					const lazyloadBackgroundObserver = new IntersectionObserver( ( entries ) => {
						entries.forEach( ( entry ) => {
							if ( entry.isIntersecting ) {
								let lazyloadBackground = entry.target;
								if( lazyloadBackground ) {
									lazyloadBackground.classList.add( 'e-lazyloaded' );
								}
								lazyloadBackgroundObserver.unobserve( entry.target );
							}
						});
					}, { rootMargin: '200px 0px 200px 0px' } );
					lazyloadBackgrounds.forEach( ( lazyloadBackground ) => {
						lazyloadBackgroundObserver.observe( lazyloadBackground );
					} );
				};
				const events = [
					'DOMContentLoaded',
					'elementor/lazyload/observe',
				];
				events.forEach( ( event ) => {
					document.addEventListener( event, lazyloadRunObserver );
				} );
			</script>

Medical Drug Detox Centers: Comprehensive Addiction Treatment Programs
 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 Drug and alcohol detox is an important first step in recovering from a substance use disorder. Supervised detox helps reduce withdrawal symptoms and cravings to support recovery. 

 Various factors, such as the severity of addiction, health status, and individual needs, influence the duration and approach of medication-assisted treatment. The benefits of medical detox include improved safety, greater comfort during withdrawal, and better long-term recovery outcomes. 

 Promises addiction treatment programs provide support for every step of the recovery journey, including detox overseen 24/7 by medical professionals. Our programs work to increase access to safe, effective detox and treatment, helping more individuals receive the care they need. 

 If you’re ready to begin recovery with a strong foundation, call 888.289.1181 to get started. 

Why Is Medical Detox Important?

 Addiction happens because drugs change how the brain functions. If you are physically dependent on a substance, you will experience withdrawal symptoms when you stop taking it. These can include headaches, insomnia, nausea, and other extremely unpleasant and/or dangerous states. 

 Supervised detox is associated with better outcomes for patients, as it reduces complications and supports long-term recovery. Medical staff tracks vital signs and can administer medications to ease your symptoms. They can also intervene if any serious problems arise. 

What Is Medication-Assisted Treatment?

 Medication-assisted treatment (MAT) is an evidence-based approach that offers hope and healing for those struggling with addictions such as opioid use disorder and alcohol use disorder. MAT uses FDA-approved medications to help minimize cravings, reduce withdrawal symptoms, and/or block the rewarding effects of substances during alcohol or opioid addiction treatment. 

 MAT words best as part of a comprehensive treatment approach. At Promises treatment centers, we address not just the physical aspects of addiction, but also the emotional, social, and psychological challenges you face. Through medications, group work, and behavioral therapies, we can help you start to regain control of your health and reclaim your life. 

Benefits of MAT in Addiction Treatment

 It’s important to understand that medication-assisted treatment (MAT) is not simply replacing one drug with another; rather, it is a carefully managed medical approach using FDA-approved medications to support recovery. 

 MAT brings hope and genuine relief to individuals facing opioid use disorder and other substance use challenges. Effective medical detox prepares you to enter alcohol or drug rehab, where you can start to explore the reasons behind the addiction. Medications for opioid use or alcohol use can reduce the risk of relapse as part of a comprehensive treatment plan. 

Medications that Treat Opioid Use Disorder and Alcohol Addiction

 There are several FDA-approved medications that can help make detox more comfortable and are used to treat opioid use disorder or alcohol addiction as part of a comprehensive treatment plan: 

 

 Benzodiazepines: can help ease withdrawal symptoms for alcohol detox 

 Buprenorphine: partial opioid agonist; blocks opioid receptors to relieve opioid cravings and reduces alcohol cravings 

 Suboxone: used for opioid use disorder; helps reduce cravings and prevent misuse 

 Naltrexone: opioid antagonist; blocks opioid receptors and prevents rewarding effects to reduce cravings for opioids and alcohol 

 Methadone: synthetic opioid agonist; eases withdrawal symptoms of heroin and opioids; used for severe addiction cases 

 

 Healthcare providers carefully prescribe these life-changing medications. They monitor effects closely and adjust treatment to ensure safety and comfort. 

Importance of Counseling in Recovery

 Counseling forms the heart of MAT. We believe it’s essential for achieving lasting recovery from substance use disorders. Behavioral therapies like cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) and contingency management help you identify and change harmful thought patterns and behaviors and give you practical tools to manage cravings, cope with stress, and prevent relapse. Group counseling and family therapy back up the individual therapy process and strengthen your support network. 

Barriers to Accessing Evidence-Based Treatment

 Medication-assisted treatment (MAT) transforms, and even saves, lives. Yet many people struggling with opioid use disorder face real challenges in accessing this essential care. Too few healthcare providers receive training to prescribe medications like naltrexone and buprenorphine. This shortage leaves many people without the treatment they desperately need and deserve. 

 Stigma and discrimination create additional hurdles for those seeking help, and some communities lack nearby treatment programs. This forces people to travel great distances or go without care entirely. We believe that increasing access to evidence-based medications and compassionate care will help us address the opioid crisis. Every person deserves the chance to heal and recover. 

Promises Medication-Assisted Treatment Locations

 We are expanding access to evidence-based treatment through our seven drug and alcohol rehab centers: 

 The Ranch in Nunnelly, TN 
 Our serene, nature-focused treatment center offers evidence-based therapies and medical detox in a peaceful rural setting, ideal for those seeking a comprehensive recovery experience. 

 The Ranch in Wrightsville, PA 
 Specializing in dual diagnosis treatment, The Ranch provides integrated care for co-occurring mental health and substance use disorders with personalized medical and therapeutic support. 

 The Right Step in Houston, TX 
 Offering inpatient drug rehab with a focus on individualized treatment plans, The Right Step Houston combines medication-assisted treatment with counseling to support lasting recovery. 

 Washburn House in Worcester, MA 
 Washburn House is a clinical drug rehab center that emphasizes evidence-based care and compassionate support, helping clients safely detox and transition into ongoing addiction treatment. 

 Promises Brazos Valley in College Station, TX 
 Providing a full spectrum of addiction services, this center offers medical detox and medication-assisted treatment alongside behavioral therapies in a supportive environment. 

 Promises Atlanta in Atlanta, GA 
 Promises in Atlanta is a comprehensive treatment center delivering personalized care with a focus on medication-assisted treatment and holistic recovery approaches tailored to individual needs. 

 Promises Dallas in Dallas, TX 
 Our Dallas location integrates medical detox, medication-assisted treatment, and therapy services to support clients through every stage of their recovery journey. 

Insurance Coverage for MAT

 Most health insurance plans now provide at least partial coverage for MAT as part of substance use disorder treatment. The Affordable Care Act (ACA) requires insurance plans to cover essential health benefits, which includes treatment for substance use disorders and access to MAT. The Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA) guarantees that coverage for substance use disorder treatment, including MAT, receives the same level of support as other medical and surgical care. 

Reach out to an Evidence-Based Detox Center

 Promises Behavioral Health is a family of addiction and mental health treatment centers offering accredited medical detox programs for a safer, more comfortable drug withdrawal and treatment experience. Our treatment centers are home-like, restorative settings that also provide the latest evidence-based treatment and medications. If you’re ready to restore your physical and mental health with the aim of long-term recovery, call 888.483.7451 today.

Dual Diagnosis Treatment & Rehab Centers

Effective drug addiction treatment tackles the root causes of dependence. For many, these causes include depression, anxiety, or trauma. To maintain sobriety, it is crucial to include dual diagnosis treatment in your long-term recovery plan. This approach fosters healing from both the addiction and any underlying mental health issues.

Promises Behavioral Health provides an array of treatment services that can be tailored to your individual needs. In our substance abuse treatment centers in Massachusetts, Tennessee, Georgia, Texas, and Pennsylvania, clients participate in dual diagnosis therapy that allows for in-depth exploration of the issues underlying their addiction. Through individual and group sessions, we help clients identify triggers and develop long-term strategies to maintain sobriety.

If you are ready to begin your journey to recovery, call our team at 888.483.7451 today to learn more about our addiction treatment programs.

Understanding Dual Diagnosis

Mental health issues play a large role in many addictions. When you struggle with both a substance use disorder and a mental health condition, this is known as a dual diagnosis or co-occurring disorders. Some of the mental health concerns that can co-occur with substance use disorders include:

  • A mood disorder, including major depression or bipolar disorder
  • Generalized anxiety disorder and other anxiety disorders
  • Obsessive-compulsive disorder
  • Borderline personality disorder
  • ADHD
  • Trauma and PTSD

Many people use alcohol or drugs to cope with an untreated mental health disorder. Drugs and alcohol may temporarily numb emotional pain, but they cannot eliminate it. Alcohol use disorder and drug abuse can develop in these cases. A dual diagnosis treatment center provides the integrated treatment you need to address both addiction and co-occurring substance and mental health conditions.

Why Dual Diagnosis Treatment Centers Are Key to Addiction Recovery

Treatment for dual diagnosis addresses both substance use disorders and related mental health conditions. Some people receiving treatment for addiction may not get the right care for a co-occurring disorder. Without proper support, one condition can aggravate the other. Without treatment, you may find yourself in a cycle of substance use that is hard to break.

Co-occurring disorder treatment programs offer clients a clear, individualized treatment plan. When clinically appropriate, Promises Behavioral Health teams coordinate referrals to relevant services in your hometown after discharge. When issues such as trauma, depression, and anxiety are left unaddressed, the risk of relapse increases. If you have co-occurring disorders and you only tackle the substance use, you may return home and feel compelled to self-medicate.

Using drugs, drinking alcohol, or engaging in other compulsive behaviors may dull symptoms of a mental health disorder for a short time, but the symptoms usually return, and substance use can worsen mental health.

How Dual Diagnosis Addiction Treatment Works

Dual diagnosis treatment is an integrated treatment approach that screens for a substance use disorder and a mental health disorder at the same time, then creates one plan for both. At Promises Behavioral Health, your plan is individualized, compassionate, and designed to support real recovery.

Key steps you can expect:

  1. Comprehensive assessment, including medical, psychiatric, and psychosocial history
  2. Safety planning when needed for suicidal thoughts or self-harm concerns
  3. A coordinated treatment approach that addresses mental health and substance use together
  4. Ongoing measurement of progress, with adjustments as your needs change
  5. Discharge planning and connections to support groups, outpatient therapy, or community resources

Common Co-Occurring Mental Health and Substance Use Disorders

Substance use can overlap with many mental disorders. While every client is unique, frequently seen combinations include:

  • Depression with alcohol use disorder
  • Anxiety disorders with stimulant or cannabis use
  • PTSD with opioid or alcohol misuse
  • Personality disorders with polysubstance use
  • Bipolar and other mood disorders with alcohol or sedative use
  • ADHD with nicotine or stimulant misuse

Untreated combinations can raise stress, impair sleep, and increase risk of relapse. Some people, including young adults, may be at increased risk during life transitions, so early support matters.

Therapies and Services Used in Dual Diagnosis Treatment

Evidence-Based Behavioral Therapies

Your clinician may use cognitive behavioral therapy, dialectical behavior skills training, and trauma-informed therapy to help you understand thoughts, emotions, and behaviors linked to substance use. Family therapy sessions can improve communication, reduce blame, and strengthen support systems.

Medication and Psychiatric Services

For some mental disorders, medications can ease symptoms so you can focus on therapy. Care is coordinated with psychiatric services to monitor benefits and side effects. Medication is one tool within a broader plan, not a stand-alone solution.

Holistic Supports and Relapse Prevention

You may practice mindfulness, fitness, and sleep hygiene, learn craving management skills, and build a relapse prevention plan. Peer and alumni connections, along with support groups in your community, can reinforce positive changes after formal care.

Inpatient and Outpatient Programs for Addiction and Mental Health Treatment

Our treatment centers offer both inpatient and outpatient treatment programs for co-occurring disorders. The right one for you depends on your needs and personal circumstances.

Inpatient Treatment

When you choose an inpatient program:

  • You stay in the rehab center.
  • A rehab team is available 24/7 to offer medical and emotional support.
  • The treatment lasts one to six months.
  • Treatment is designed for severe cases of drug or alcohol addiction.
  • There are fewer distractions from daily life.

Outpatient Treatment

When it comes to an outpatient program:

  • You live at home and attend treatment during the day or evening.
  • You maintain a more normal routine compared to an inpatient program.
  • The treatment lasts three months to one year.
  • The plan can be suited to those who have already completed residential care.
  • You spend at least 10 to 12 hours a week at the outpatient rehab center.

Outpatient care is less restrictive than inpatient care, but residential treatment may be necessary for someone who needs close monitoring during medically managed detox or who has significant safety concerns.

Overcoming Stigma to Find Help

One barrier to dual diagnosis is stigma. Some people mistakenly view addiction as a failure of willpower and mental illness as weakness. These beliefs overlook how mental health and substance challenges often reinforce one another. Our teams understand that shame is a common part of the experience, and we prioritize respectful, person-first language and confidential care every step of the way.

What to Expect at Promises Behavioral Health

From the first call, our clinicians take time to understand your history, symptoms, and goals. You will receive coordinated care that addresses both a substance use disorder and a mental health disorder. If you want to explore our continuum of care in Massachusetts, Tennessee, Georgia, Texas, or Pennsylvania, you can learn more about our mental health treatment programs and how they align with addiction care.

Warning Signs and When to Seek Help

Reach out for help if you notice:

  • Escalating drug use or alcohol use to cope with emotions
  • Frequent panic, hopelessness, or swings in mood
  • Social withdrawal, missed responsibilities, or unsafe behaviors
  • Thoughts of self harm or suicidal thoughts

For any immediate danger, call 911. You can also call 988 for the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline for urgent support. After you are safe, we can help you plan next steps for ongoing care.

How Families Can Support a Loved One

Families can be powerful partners in recovery. Helpful steps include:

  • Learning about co-occurring disorders and how symptoms interact
  • Practicing calm, nonjudgmental communication
  • Setting healthy boundaries, such as clear expectations about substance use in the home
  • Encouraging follow-up appointments and medication adherence when prescribed
  • Taking care of your own well-being

For tools you can use today, visit the Promises Family Resource Center.

Reach Out to Promises Behavioral Health for Dual Diagnosis Treatment

If a loved one is struggling with both substance abuse and a mental health condition, dual diagnosis treatment can help them regain stability and confidence. Contact our team at 888.483.7451 for a confidential conversation, or learn more about our mission and values on the About Promises Behavioral Health page. We are here to help you take the next step toward real recovery.

Frequently asked questions

What are dual diagnosis treatment centers and how do they help individuals with co-occurring disorders?

Dual diagnosis treatment centers specialize in treating individuals who have both mental health disorders and substance use disorders, providing integrated care to address both conditions simultaneously for better recovery outcomes.

How can I find dual diagnosis treatment centers near me that offer inpatient or residential programs?

You can locate dual diagnosis treatment centers near you by searching online directories, contacting local mental health organizations, or calling helplines that connect you with nearby rehab, inpatient, and residential programs tailored to co-occurring disorders.

What is the difference between dual diagnosis rehab centers and dual diagnosis inpatient treatment centers?
Dual diagnosis rehab centers may offer a range of treatment options, including outpatient and inpatient care, while dual diagnosis inpatient treatment centers specifically provide 24/7 supervised care in a structured environment for individuals needing intensive support.
 
What types of therapies are offered in dual diagnosis treatment programs, such as counseling and group therapy?

Dual diagnosis treatment programs typically include individual counseling, group therapy, medication management, and holistic therapies, all designed to address both mental health and substance use issues in a comprehensive manner.

Are there specialized dual diagnosis treatment centers in locations like Florida and Texas, and what services do they provide?

Yes, there are specialized dual diagnosis treatment centers in states like Florida and Texas that offer a variety of services, including detox, inpatient and residential care, counseling, group therapy, and aftercare planning for individuals with co-occurring disorders.

Treatment for dual diagnosis addresses both substance use disorders and related mental health conditions. Some people receiving treatment for addiction may not get the right care for a co-occurring disorder. Without proper support, one condition can aggravate the other. Without treatment, you may find yourself in a cycle of substance use that is hard to break.

Co-occurring disorder treatment programs offer clients a clear, individualized treatment plan. When clinically appropriate, Promises Behavioral Health teams coordinate referrals to relevant services in your hometown after discharge. When issues such as trauma, depression, and anxiety are left unaddressed, the risk of relapse increases. If you have co-occurring disorders and you only tackle the substance use, you may return home and feel compelled to self-medicate.

Using drugs, drinking alcohol, or engaging in other compulsive behaviors may dull symptoms of a mental health disorder for a short time, but the symptoms usually return, and substance use can worsen mental health.

How Dual Diagnosis Addiction Treatment Works

Dual diagnosis treatment is an integrated treatment approach that screens for a substance use disorder and a mental health disorder at the same time, then creates one plan for both. At Promises Behavioral Health, your plan is individualized, compassionate, and designed to support real recovery.

Key steps you can expect:
 
Comprehensive assessment, including medical, psychiatric, and psychosocial history

Safety planning when needed for suicidal thoughts or self-harm concerns

A coordinated treatment approach that addresses mental health and substance use together

Ongoing measurement of progress, with adjustments as your needs change

Discharge planning and connections to support groups, outpatient therapy, or community resources
 
Common Co-Occurring Mental Health and Substance Use Disorders

Substance use can overlap with many mental disorders. While every client is unique, frequently seen combinations include:
 
Depression with alcohol use disorder

Anxiety disorders with stimulant or cannabis use

PTSD with opioid or alcohol misuse

Personality disorders with polysubstance use

Bipolar and other mood disorders with alcohol or sedative use

ADHD with nicotine or stimulant misuse
 
Untreated combinations can raise stress, impair sleep, and increase risk of relapse. Some people, including young adults, may be at increased risk during life transitions, so early support matters.

Therapies and Services Used in Dual Diagnosis Treatment

Evidence-Based Behavioral Therapies

Your clinician may use cognitive behavioral therapy, dialectical behavior skills training, and trauma-informed therapy to help you understand thoughts, emotions, and behaviors linked to substance use. Family therapy sessions can improve communication, reduce blame, and strengthen support systems.

Medication and Psychiatric Services

For some mental disorders, medications can ease symptoms so you can focus on therapy. Care is coordinated with psychiatric services to monitor benefits and side effects. Medication is one tool within a broader plan, not a stand-alone solution.

Holistic Supports and Relapse Prevention

You may practice mindfulness, fitness, and sleep hygiene, learn craving management skills, and build a relapse prevention plan. Peer and alumni connections, along with support groups in your community, can reinforce positive changes after formal care.

Inpatient and Outpatient Programs for Addiction and Mental Health Treatment

Our treatment centers offer both inpatient and outpatient treatment programs for co-occurring disorders. The right one for you depends on your needs and personal circumstances.

Inpatient Treatment

When you choose an inpatient program:
 
You stay in the rehab center.

A rehab team is available 24/7 to offer medical and emotional support.

The treatment lasts one to six months.

Treatment is designed for severe cases of drug or alcohol addiction.

There are fewer distractions from daily life.
 
Outpatient Treatment

When it comes to an outpatient program:
 
You live at home and attend treatment during the day or evening.

You maintain a more normal routine compared to an inpatient program.

The treatment lasts three months to one year.

The plan can be suited to those who have already completed residential care.

You spend at least 10 to 12 hours a week at the outpatient rehab center.
 
Outpatient care is less restrictive than inpatient care, but residential treatment may be necessary for someone who needs close monitoring during medically managed detox or who has significant safety concerns.

Overcoming Stigma to Find Help

One barrier to dual diagnosis is stigma. Some people mistakenly view addiction as a failure of willpower and mental illness as weakness. These beliefs overlook how mental health and substance challenges often reinforce one another. Our teams understand that shame is a common part of the experience, and we prioritize respectful, person-first language and confidential care every step of the way.

What to Expect at Promises Behavioral Health

From the first call, our clinicians take time to understand your history, symptoms, and goals. You will receive coordinated care that addresses both a substance use disorder and a mental health disorder. If you want to explore our continuum of care in Massachusetts, Tennessee, Georgia, Texas, or Pennsylvania, you can learn more about our mental health treatment programs and how they align with addiction care.

Warning Signs and When to Seek Help

Reach out for help if you notice:
 
Escalating drug use or alcohol use to cope with emotions

Frequent panic, hopelessness, or swings in mood

Social withdrawal, missed responsibilities, or unsafe behaviors

Thoughts of self harm or suicidal thoughts
 
For any immediate danger, call 911. You can also call 988 for the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline for urgent support. After you are safe, we can help you plan next steps for ongoing care.

How Families Can Support a Loved One

Families can be powerful partners in recovery. Helpful steps include:
 
Learning about co-occurring disorders and how symptoms interact

Practicing calm, nonjudgmental communication

Setting healthy boundaries, such as clear expectations about substance use in the home

Encouraging follow-up appointments and medication adherence when prescribed

Taking care of your own well-being
 
For tools you can use today, visit the Promises Family Resource Center .

Reach Out to Promises Behavioral Health for Dual Diagnosis Treatment

If a loved one is struggling with both substance abuse and a mental health condition, dual diagnosis treatment can help them regain stability and confidence. Contact our team at 888.483.7451 for a confidential conversation, or learn more about our mission and values on the About Promises Behavioral Health page. We are here to help you take the next step toward real recovery.
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Frequently asked questions
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 What are dual diagnosis treatment centers and how do they help individuals with co-occurring disorders? 
 

 

Dual diagnosis treatment centers specialize in treating individuals who have both mental health disorders and substance use disorders, providing integrated care to address both conditions simultaneously for better recovery outcomes.

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 How can I find dual diagnosis treatment centers near me that offer inpatient or residential programs? 
 

 

You can locate dual diagnosis treatment centers near you by searching online directories, contacting local mental health organizations, or calling helplines that connect you with nearby rehab, inpatient, and residential programs tailored to co-occurring disorders.

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 What is the difference between dual diagnosis rehab centers and dual diagnosis inpatient treatment centers? 
 

 

Dual diagnosis rehab centers may offer a range of treatment options, including outpatient and inpatient care, while dual diagnosis inpatient treatment centers specifically provide 24/7 supervised care in a structured environment for individuals needing intensive support.

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 What types of therapies are offered in dual diagnosis treatment programs, such as counseling and group therapy? 
 

 

Dual diagnosis treatment programs typically include individual counseling, group therapy, medication management, and holistic therapies, all designed to address both mental health and substance use issues in a comprehensive manner.

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 Are there specialized dual diagnosis treatment centers in locations like Florida and Texas, and what services do they provide? 
 

 

Yes, there are specialized dual diagnosis treatment centers in states like Florida and Texas that offer a variety of services, including detox, inpatient and residential care, counseling, group therapy, and aftercare planning for individuals with co-occurring disorders.

						<section class="elementor-section elementor-top-section elementor-element elementor-element-5a7bd645 elementor-section-boxed elementor-section-height-default elementor-section-height-default" data-id="5a7bd645" data-element_type="section">

						<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-c8b7c3e elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="c8b7c3e" data-element_type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
				<div class="elementor-widget-container">
									<p><img fetchpriority="high" decoding="async" class="alignright wp-image-7579 size-medium" src="https://www.promisesbehavioralhealth.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/04/Outpatient-Drug-Rehab-Centers-1-300x200.jpeg" alt="a man gets help in outpatient drug rehab centers" width="300" height="200" srcset="https://www.promises.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/04/Outpatient-Drug-Rehab-Centers-1-300x200.jpeg 300w, https://www.promises.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/04/Outpatient-Drug-Rehab-Centers-1.jpeg 500w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" /><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Promises Behavioral Health, our addiction treatment centers provide a full range of options for individuals ready to begin recovery. For many, outpatient drug rehab is the right fit. These programs offer professional care and guidance without requiring you to live at a facility full-time, making it possible to balance treatment with work, school, and family life.</span></p><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Our</span><a href="https://www.promises.com/addiction-treatment-center-programs/outpatient-drug-rehabilitation-centers/" data-wpel-link="internal"> <span style="font-weight: 400;">outpatient addiction treatment programs</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> in Texas, Pennsylvania, Massachusetts, and Georgia are designed to meet you where you are. Whether you are just beginning the recovery process or continuing treatment after a residential stay, we’ll help you create a plan that fits your needs. With expert providers, flexible schedules, and a compassionate community, outpatient rehab makes recovery possible without putting the rest of your life on hold.</span></p><h2>How Outpatient Addiction Treatment Works</h2><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">In outpatient drug rehab, clients participate in individual therapy, group sessions, and other structured services during the day or evening, then return home or to a sober living residence at night. This approach allows you to stay engaged with daily commitments while receiving the guidance and accountability that foster lasting recovery.</span></p><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Key features of outpatient rehab include:</span></p><ul><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">A flexible schedule that supports patients who work or attend school</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Individualized treatment planning to address unique needs</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Access to medical and psychiatric services when needed</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">A supportive environment that promotes personal growth and self-esteem</span></li></ul><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Outpatient rehab can be an effective choice at different stages of recovery. Some people begin their journey with outpatient services, while others transition to them after completing inpatient care.</span></p><h2>Levels of Care in Outpatient Rehab</h2><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Not all outpatient programs are the same. At Promises, we offer different levels of intensity to ensure you receive the right balance of structure and flexibility:</span></p><h4>Partial Hospitalization Programs</h4><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">PHPs are the most intensive outpatient option, requiring a significant time commitment: often five days a week for several hours each day. PHPs are ideal for patients who need strong clinical support but can live safely at home.</span></p><h4>Intensive Outpatient Programs</h4><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">A step down from PHPs, IOPs usually meet several days a week for multiple hours per session. This form of intensive treatment provides structure while giving you time to manage other responsibilities.</span></p><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We also offer</span><a href="https://www.promises.com/substance-abuse-treatment-programs/residential-alcohol-addiction-treatment-rehab-centers/" data-wpel-link="internal"> <span style="font-weight: 400;">residential rehab</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> for those who need more intensive, 24/7 support. This continuum of care makes it possible to adjust treatment as your needs evolve.</span></p><h2>Therapies and Services in Outpatient Treatment</h2><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Our outpatient programs at Promises use a variety of evidence-based approaches to address both substance abuse and co-occurring mental health conditions. This includes:</span></p><ul><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Individual therapy</b><span style="font-weight: 400;"> to focus on personal challenges and recovery goals</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Group therapy</b><span style="font-weight: 400;"> to foster peer accountability and shared learning</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Family therapy</b><span style="font-weight: 400;"> to strengthen relationships and improve communication</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Relapse prevention skills</b><span style="font-weight: 400;"> training to lower the risk of setbacks</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Psychiatric services</b><span style="font-weight: 400;"> for clients managing mental illness alongside addiction</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Community support</b><span style="font-weight: 400;"> through recovery groups and sober living resources</span></li></ul><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">These therapies and supports can help you identify unhealthy behaviors, build community, and develop healthier ways to handle life’s challenges.</span></p><h2>Who Benefits from Outpatient Programs?</h2><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Outpatient programs are especially helpful for:</span></p><ul><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">People balancing treatment with work, school, or family responsibilities</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Clients who have completed detox or residential care and are ready for continued support</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Individuals with strong support systems at home</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Those looking for affordable alternatives to residential treatment</span></li></ul><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Most patients in outpatient rehab benefit from the support network they build along the way. By engaging in therapy and connecting with others, you learn practical strategies for recovery and strengthen your confidence to manage life outside of treatment.</span></p><h2>Who Should Consider Inpatient Care Instead?</h2><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Outpatient rehab isn’t the best fit for everyone. You may need an inpatient program if:</span></p><ul><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">You have a history of repeated relapse or severe substance abuse</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">You pose a danger to yourself or others without close supervision</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Your home environment is unstable or unsupportive of recovery</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">You need 24/7 monitoring to manage withdrawal or serious health problems</span></li></ul><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">In these situations, inpatient rehab provides the daily structure and medical attention required for safety and success.</span></p><h2>Patient Populations We Serve</h2><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Our outpatient programs are open to people from all walks of life. We customize care for:</span></p><ul><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Men, women, working professionals, and young adults managing unique recovery challenges</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Individuals with co-occurring disorders requiring integrated mental health treatment</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Clients addressing all forms of substance use disorders, from alcohol to opioids to methamphetamine</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Those navigating stressful life transitions who need flexible addiction treatment and mental health services</span></li></ul><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Because every recovery journey is different, we adapt treatment to your circumstances, goals, and responsibilities.</span></p><h2>Promises Outpatient Rehab Locations</h2><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Promises Behavioral Health provides outpatient drug rehab services at centers across the country, giving you access to quality addiction treatment close to home:</span></p><p><a href="https://www.promises.com/promises-drug-addiction-treatment-centers/the-ranch-nunnelly-tn-evidence-based-treatment-center/" data-wpel-link="internal"><b>The Ranch in Nunnelly, TN</b><b><br /></b></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">Located in the serene countryside of Tennessee outside Nashville, The Ranch offers evidence-based outpatient treatment programs that combine individual therapy, group sessions, and family therapy to foster lasting recovery in a supportive environment.</span></p><p><a href="https://www.promises.com/promises-drug-addiction-treatment-centers/the-ranch-wrightsville-pa-dual-diagnosis-treatment-center/" data-wpel-link="internal"><b>The Ranch in Wrightsville, PA</b><b><br /></b></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">Serving the York and Lancaster communities, The Ranch specializes in dual diagnosis treatment, addressing both substance use disorders and co-occurring mental health conditions with comprehensive outpatient care.</span></p><p><a href="https://www.promises.com/promises-drug-addiction-treatment-centers/the-right-step-houston-tx-inpatient-drug-rehab-center/" data-wpel-link="internal"><b>The Right Step in Houston, TX</b><b><br /></b></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">The Right Step in Houston provides intensive outpatient programs designed to accommodate busy schedules while delivering effective addiction treatment, including medical support and relapse prevention education.</span></p><p><a href="https://www.promises.com/promises-drug-addiction-treatment-centers/washburn-house-worcester-ma-clinical-drug-rehab-center/" data-wpel-link="internal"><b>Washburn House in Worcester, MA</b><b><br /></b></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">Situated in Worcester County, Washburn House offers clinical outpatient drug rehab services focusing on personalized treatment planning and integration of psychiatric services for clients with co-occurring disorders.</span></p><p><a href="https://www.promises.com/promises-drug-addiction-treatment-centers/promises-brazos-valley/" data-wpel-link="internal"><b>Promises Brazos Valley in College Station, TX</b><b><br /></b></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">Promises Brazos Valley combines outpatient services with community resources to support recovery, emphasizing coping strategies, behavioral changes, and building a strong support system for our clients in College Station.</span></p><p><a href="https://www.promisesatlanta.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener follow external noreferrer" data-wpel-link="external"><b>Promises in Atlanta, GA</b><b><br /></b></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">In Atlanta, Promises provides flexible outpatient programs that include group therapy, individual counseling, and family involvement to help clients maintain recovery while managing daily responsibilities.</span></p><p><a href="https://www.promisesdfw.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener follow external noreferrer" data-wpel-link="external"><b>Promises in Dallas, TX</b><b><br /></b></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">Promises Dallas offers comprehensive outpatient addiction treatment tailored to meet diverse needs, integrating therapeutic interventions and support groups to promote sustained sobriety.</span></p><h2>Take the First Step Toward Recovery</h2><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Choosing outpatient rehab is a courageous step toward building a healthier life. At Promises Behavioral Health, we’ll help you create a complete treatment plan that addresses your needs and supports your goals. Our dedicated providers and staff walk with you through every stage of the recovery process, from early treatment to ongoing aftercare.</span></p><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Call us today at 888.289.1181 or</span><a href="https://www.promises.com/contact-promises-behavioral-health/" data-wpel-link="internal"> <span style="font-weight: 400;">reach out online</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> to learn more about our outpatient drug rehab centers and start your journey toward lasting recovery.</span></p>								</div>

				const lazyloadRunObserver = () => {
					const lazyloadBackgrounds = document.querySelectorAll( `.e-con.e-parent:not(.e-lazyloaded)` );
					const lazyloadBackgroundObserver = new IntersectionObserver( ( entries ) => {
						entries.forEach( ( entry ) => {
							if ( entry.isIntersecting ) {
								let lazyloadBackground = entry.target;
								if( lazyloadBackground ) {
									lazyloadBackground.classList.add( 'e-lazyloaded' );
								}
								lazyloadBackgroundObserver.unobserve( entry.target );
							}
						});
					}, { rootMargin: '200px 0px 200px 0px' } );
					lazyloadBackgrounds.forEach( ( lazyloadBackground ) => {
						lazyloadBackgroundObserver.observe( lazyloadBackground );
					} );
				};
				const events = [
					'DOMContentLoaded',
					'elementor/lazyload/observe',
				];
				events.forEach( ( event ) => {
					document.addEventListener( event, lazyloadRunObserver );
				} );
			</script>
